title: Coelogyne niana
intro: Coelogyne niana, also known as the "Malaysian Coelogyne," is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate respiratory issues and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Coelogyne niana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Coelogyne niana is often prepared as a tea or dried extract, and is used to treat a variety of conditions, including bronchitis, coughs, and fever.
taxonomy: Coelogyne niana is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family includes approximately 30,000 species of orchids, making it one of the largest plant families. Coelogyne niana is a genus within the subfamily Epidendroideae, a group of orchids that are mostly terrestrial and have pseudobulbs. The genus Coelogyne is a section within the tribe Coelogyneae, which is known to have approximately 200 species. These classifications are used to understand the evolutionary relationships and diversity within the plant kingdom.
